ops.title: User wants minimal comments — code should be self-documenting; do not add verbose explanatory comments.
ops.body:
The user pushed back on verbose multi-line explanatory comments in generated code: "stop adding super verbose comments; code should be self-documenting."

**Why:** It matches the repo's own STYLEGUIDE.md — "Code documents itself through names, types, and organization. No comments except TODOs, non-obvious workarounds, or disabled code." Internal helpers get no docstrings; a docstring that restates the signature is clutter to delete. Workflow subagents tend to over-comment (module `//!` blocks, `///` on internal helpers, inline "why" narration) — that must be trimmed before landing.

**How to apply:** Lean on names/types/structure. Reserve comments for TODOs (incl. `TODO(phaseN)` markers), genuinely non-obvious workarounds, or disabled code. Keep public-API docstrings minimal and only where the styleguide wants them. Never narrate what the code already says. When dispatching codegen agents, instruct them to follow the no-comments rule. See [[design-exploration-mode]] for the related "match the user's register" theme.
